texts.full_text: by a BSD-style // license that can be found in the LICENSE file. // +build aix // +build ppc64 package unix //sysnb Getrlimit(resource int, rlim *Rlimit) (err error) //sysnb Setrlimit(resource int, rlim *Rlimit) (err error) //sys Seek(fd int, offset int64, whence int) (off int64, err error) = lseek //sys mmap(addr uintptr, length uintptr, prot int, flags int, fd int, offset int64) (xaddr uintptr, err error) = mmap64 func setTimespec(sec, nsec int64) Timespec { return Timespec{Sec: sec, Nsec: nsec} } func setTimeval(sec, usec int64) Timeval { return Timeval{Sec: int64(sec), Usec: int32(usec)} } func (iov *Iovec) SetLen(length int) { iov.Len = uint64(length) } func (msghdr *Msghdr) SetControllen(length int) { msghdr.Controllen = uint32(length) } func (msghdr *Msghdr) SetIovlen(length int) { msghdr.Iovlen = int32(length) } func (cmsg *Cmsghdr) SetLen(length int) { cmsg.Len = uint32(length) } // In order to only have Timespec structure, type of Stat_t's fields // Atim, Mtim and Ctim is changed from StTimespec to Timespec during /// the transition from 32-bit to 64-bit time representation. // Stat_t is a structure that represents file status information. type Stat_t struct { Dev uint64 Ino uint64 Nlink uint64 Mode uint32 Uid uint32 Gid uint32 Rdev uint64 Size int64 Blksize int64 Blocks int64 Atim Timespec Mtim Timespec Ctim Timespec // Additional fields can be added here } // Timespec represents a time with nanosecond precision. type Timespec struct { Sec int64 Nsec int64 } // Timeval represents a time with microsecond precision. type Timeval struct { Sec int64 Usec int32 } // Iovec represents a scatter/gather I/O vector. type Iovec struct { Base *byte Len uint64 } // Msghdr represents a message header for sendmsg and recvmsg. type Msghdr struct { Name Iovec Iovlen int32 Control *byte Controllen uint32 Flags int32 } // Cmsghdr represents

texts.full_text: Bristol City. Bristol City reached the semifinals of that season's competition before losing to Newcastle United. In his first full campaign, Rodgers was primarily used as cover at centre half and also occasionally played right back or even left mid-fielder depending on who else would be playing in those positions (e.g. when either Andy Watson - the regular Right Back – moved up front for a partnership with Eric Delgado). He made his first team debut against Crewe Alexandra, where he started at centre half and went off after only twenty minutes due to injury. The following season saw him play in eighteen matches (seven of them as substitute) which included two goals. He scored the second goal for Bristol City when they beat Huddersfield Town 2-0 on New Year's Day, his first competitive match that year and he also netted a last minute equaliser against West Bromwich Albion at home in front of over twenty thousand spectators. He was primarily used as cover to the regular centre halves (Maurice Ingram & Andy Watson) until 1973 when Mauricio Pochettino became first choice. The next season saw him play nineteen matches and he scored two goals again, including a winner against Manchester City at Maine Road which remains one of his best ever performances. He had another successful campaign in the following year where he made twenty-three appearances (including thirteen as substitute) with four more league
